edges.description: The rearrangement places CCND1 under enhancer control and drives constitutive cyclin D1 expression.
Excess cyclin D1 deregulates the G1 checkpoint and promotes entry into S phase.
Faster cell-cycle transit increases accumulation of neoplastic B cells.
Loss of checkpoint control permits ongoing accumulation of structural and sequence alterations.
Genetically complex clones gain fitness advantages that support continued accumulation of lymphoma cells.
Niche-dependent cues reinforce ongoing B-cell receptor signaling in mantle cell lymphoma cells.
edges.source: Microenvironmental crosstalk in marrow and secondary lymphoid organs

edges.description: BCR pathway output sustains survival signaling in neoplastic B lymphocytes.
Reduced cell death allows continued net accumulation of lymphoma cells.
Progressive clonal accumulation ultimately presents as advanced-stage nodal and extranodal disease.
edges.target: Widespread tissue infiltration by malignant B cells

edges.description: Disseminated nodal accumulation of lymphoma cells manifests clinically as widespread lymph node enlargement.
edges.source: Widespread tissue infiltration by malignant B cells

edges.description: Lymphomatous splenic involvement produces clinically apparent enlargement of the spleen.
edges.source: Widespread tissue infiltration by malignant B cells

edges.description: Extranodal infiltration of the gut manifests as mantle cell lymphoma involvement of the gastrointestinal tract.
edges.source: Widespread tissue infiltration by malignant B cells

nodes.description: Second-generation selective BTK inhibitor with efficacy in relapsed MCL and potentially improved tolerability compared to ibrutinib.

evidence.snippet: The purpose of this study is to characterize the safety and efficacy profile of ACP-196 (acalabrutinib) in subjects with relapsed or refractory Mantle Cell Lymphoma (MCL).

nodes.description: Activated B-cell receptor signaling is a central prosurvival program in mantle cell lymphoma and helps integrate external cues from the tumor microenvironment.

evidence.reference_title: B-cell receptor signaling and microenvironment crosstalk in mantle cell lymphoma.
evidence.snippet: This dynamic and complex system, including the activation of B-cell receptor signaling (BCR), promotes survival, immune evasion, and therapeutic resistance.

nodes.description: Integrated prosurvival signaling reduces apoptotic restraint and helps mantle cell lymphoma clones persist within supportive tissue niches.

evidence.reference_title: B-cell receptor signaling and microenvironment crosstalk in mantle cell lymphoma.
evidence.snippet: Given the central role of the BCR signaling pathway in proliferation and survival of neoplastic B lymphocytes, in the last decades several biological agents against the key BCR proteins, i.e. Bruton Tyrosine Kinase inhibitors (BTKi), have been developed, and they represent the most effective treatment for MCL management.

nodes.description: Effective frontline regimen for older or transplant-ineligible patients. Achieves high response rates with manageable toxicity.

evidence.reference_title: Phase II study of bendamustine combined with rituximab in relapsed/refractory mantle cell lymphoma: efficacy, tolerability, and safety findings.
evidence.snippet: bendamustine-rituximab is an effective therapy with manageable toxicity in relapsed/refractory MCL.
PURPOSE: This randomized phase II trial studies rituximab, bortezomib, bendamustine, and lenalidomide in treating previously untreated older patients with mantle cell lymphoma.

nodes.description: CD19-directed CAR-T cells (brexucabtagene autoleucel) approved for relapsed/refractory MCL after BTK inhibitor failure. Achieves durable remissions in heavily pretreated patients.

evidence.snippet: The goal of this clinical study is to test how well the study drug, brexucabtagene autoleucel (KTE-X19), works in participants with relapsed/refractory (r/r) mantle cell lymphoma (MCL).

nodes.description: The defining t(11;14)(q13;q32) rearrangement juxtaposes CCND1 with immunoglobulin heavy-chain regulatory elements in mantle cell lymphoma cells.

evidence.reference_title: Pathogenesis of mantle-cell lymphoma: all oncogenic roads lead to dysregulation of cell cycle and DNA damage response pathways.
evidence.snippet: The genetic hallmark of MCL is the chromosomal translocation t(11;14)(q13;q32) that leads to deregulation and upregulation of Cyclin D1, an important regulator of the G1 phase of the cell cycle.

nodes.description: Constitutive cyclin D1 expression is the canonical biochemical consequence of the CCND1 rearrangement and removes normal restraint on G1-phase control.

evidence.reference_title: How I treat mantle cell lymphoma.
evidence.snippet: Mantle cell lymphoma is included in the World Health Organization classification as distinct lymphoma subtype characterized by the t(11;14)(q13;q32) translocation, which results in overexpression of Cyclin D1.

nodes.description: Secondary lesions involving ATM, CHK1/CHK2, TP53, and related checkpoint regulators weaken the normal response to DNA damage in mantle cell lymphoma.

evidence.reference_title: Mantle cell lymphoma: biology, pathogenesis, and the molecular basis of treatment in the genomic era.
evidence.snippet: Secondary genetic events increase the oncogenic potential of cyclin D1 and frequently inactivate DNA damage response pathways.

nodes.description: Cyclin D1-driven checkpoint escape accelerates G1-to-S progression and sustains the proliferative program of malignant mantle-zone B cells.

biological_processes: G1/S transition of mitotic cell cycle

evidence.reference_title: Pathogenesis of mantle-cell lymphoma: all oncogenic roads lead to dysregulation of cell cycle and DNA damage response pathways.
evidence.snippet: The genetic hallmark of MCL is the chromosomal translocation t(11;14)(q13;q32) that leads to deregulation and upregulation of Cyclin D1, an important regulator of the G1 phase of the cell cycle.

nodes.description: MCL has a propensity for gastrointestinal tract involvement with lymphomatous polyposis. Colonoscopy may reveal multiple polyps.

evidence.reference_title: Gastrointestinal involvement in mantle cell lymphoma: a prospective clinic, endoscopic, and pathologic study.
evidence.snippet: As a whole, 92% of patients showed upper or lower GI tract infiltration by MCL.

nodes.description: Widespread lymph node enlargement is the most common presentation. Multiple nodal sites are typically involved at diagnosis.

evidence.reference_title: Mantle cell lymphoma: diagnostic criteria, clinical aspects and therapeutic problems.
evidence.snippet: Initial parameters in all published series are advanced disease with generalized lymphadenopathy in 90%

nodes.description: Complex secondary genomic alterations accumulate as checkpoint failure and cell-cycle dysregulation reinforce clonal diversification and biologic aggressiveness.

evidence.reference_title: Mantle cell lymphoma: biology, pathogenesis, and the molecular basis of treatment in the genomic era.
evidence.snippet: Secondary genetic events increase the oncogenic potential of cyclin D1 and frequently inactivate DNA damage response pathways. In combination these changes drive cell-cycle progression and give rise to pronounced genetic instability.

evidence.reference_title: Genomic and epigenomic insights into the origin, pathogenesis, and clinical behavior of mantle cell lymphoma subtypes.
evidence.snippet: An increasing genomic complexity, together with the presence of breakage-fusion-bridge cycles and high DNA methylation changes related to the proliferative cell history, defines patients with different clinical evolution.

nodes.description: First-in-class BTK inhibitor with high activity in relapsed/refractory MCL. Achieves overall response rates of 65-70%. Also being studied in frontline combinations. Continuous therapy until progression or intolerance.

evidence.reference_title: Long-term follow-up of MCL patients treated with single-agent ibrutinib: updated safety and efficacy results.
evidence.snippet: Ibrutinib, an oral inhibitor of Bruton tyrosine kinase, is approved for patients with mantle cell lymphoma (MCL) who have received one prior therapy.

evidence.reference_title: Long-term follow-up of MCL patients treated with single-agent ibrutinib: updated safety and efficacy results.
evidence.snippet: The ORR was 67% (23% complete response), with a median duration of response of 17.5 months.

nodes.description: For younger, fit patients, intensive induction regimens (R-DHAP, R-HyperCVAD) followed by high-dose therapy and autologous stem cell transplant consolidation achieves prolonged remissions. Standard approach for transplant-eligible patients.

evidence.reference_title: How I treat mantle cell lymphoma.
evidence.snippet: Very intensive regimens, including autologous and allogeneic stem cell transplantation, seem required to improve the outcome

nodes.description: Combined cell-cycle acceleration, survival signaling, and genetic complexity support net accumulation of neoplastic mantle-zone B cells over time.

evidence.reference_title: B-cell receptor signaling and microenvironment crosstalk in mantle cell lymphoma.
evidence.snippet: Given the central role of the BCR signaling pathway in proliferation and survival of neoplastic B lymphocytes, in the last decades several biological agents against the key BCR proteins, i.e. Bruton Tyrosine Kinase inhibitors (BTKi), have been developed, and they represent the most effective treatment for MCL management.

nodes.description: Mantle cell lymphoma cells occupy supportive niches in bone marrow and secondary lymphoid organs where adhesion molecules and soluble factors shape lymphoma-cell behavior.
nodes.id: Microenvironmental crosstalk in marrow and secondary lymphoid organs

evidence.reference_title: B-cell receptor signaling and microenvironment crosstalk in mantle cell lymphoma.
evidence.snippet: MCL cells develop in specialized tissue microenvironments such as bone marrow and secondary lymphoid organs, where the pathological cells interact with several microenvironmental components through a complex network of soluble factors and adhesion molecules.

nodes.description: Spleen enlargement is common, particularly prominent in the leukemic non-nodal variant.

evidence.reference_title: The leukemic presentation of mantle-cell lymphoma: disease features and prognostic factors in 58 patients.
evidence.snippet: Presenting features included splenomegaly (74%), lymphadenopathy (45%), hepatomegaly (17%)

nodes.description: BCL2 inhibitor with activity in relapsed MCL, particularly in combination with BTK inhibitors. Being evaluated in multiple combination regimens.

evidence.snippet: The therapeutic options in MCL are constantly evolving, with dramatic responses from nonchemotherapeutic agents (ibrutinib, acalabrutinib, and venetoclax).

This Phase 3 multinational, randomized, double-blind study is designed to compare the efficacy and safety of the combination of ibrutinib and venetoclax vs. ibrutinib and placebo in subjects with MCL.

nodes.description: Mantle cell lymphoma commonly presents as advanced-stage disease with both nodal and extranodal involvement, especially in bone marrow and the gastrointestinal tract.
nodes.id: Widespread tissue infiltration by malignant B cells

evidence.reference_title: Mantle cell lymphoma: state-of-the-art management and future perspective.
evidence.snippet: Most patients present with advanced stage disease, often with extranodal dissemination, and typically pursue an aggressive clinical course.

evidence.reference_title: How I treat mantle cell lymphoma.
evidence.snippet: The clinical presentation often includes extranodal involvement, particularly of the bone marrow and gut.
